questions: What is the exact production schema for mentions in `discord_messages`? Repository reads show `attachments`, `embeds`, and `reference_id`, but I did not find a clear `mentions` column. If no column exists, should `mentions_author_id` match Discord mention text in `content` such as `<@123>` / `<@!123>`?

For the 30-day archive range cap, should an over-broad `after`/`before` interval be clamped silently as the brief says, or rejected as `tool_error` so the agent knows it asked for too much? I assume clamp unless malformed.

success_criteria.criterion: `TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS` exposes exactly one `search_messages` read tool using the unified filter schema and includes a separate `get_reply_chain` tool with required `message_id`.

The old topic-editor `search_messages` parameters `author_id`, `channel_id`, and `hours_back` are no longer part of that tool schema, and equivalent behavior is available through `from_author_id`, `in_channel_id`, `after`, and default `scope='window'`.
Window-scope `search_messages` AND-combines query, author, channel, mention, has, time, reply-status, and limit filters against `dispatcher_context['messages']`.

Archive-scope `search_messages` calls a storage-backed `search_messages_unified` path scoped by `guild_id` and `environment`, with parameterized content matching and compact result rows capped to safe fields/previews.
Malformed `after` or `before` values produce a `tool_error` outcome rather than being ignored.
`get_reply_chain` walks `discord_messages.reference_id` iteratively, clamps `max_depth` to 15, detects cycles, and returns compact ancestor rows root-first.

The topic-editor system prompt documents the unified search examples and `get_reply_chain` usage without modifying `src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py` or `src/features/summarising/live_update_prompts.py`.
Focused topic-editor tests pass after updating existing `search_messages` tests and adding coverage for archive dispatch, filter combinations, reply-chain walking, and cycle detection.

No guarded flows are changed: `finalize_run`, the agent loop, override-collision, idempotency, lease-expiry, force-close, and vision tool behavior remain outside the diff except incidental imports/schema lists if required.
Implementation keeps search/reply helpers small and direct, with no raw SQL string interpolation for user-provided search text.
Result payloads remain compact enough for the existing ~2KB tool-result cap, with `content_preview` capped at 200 characters per message.

assumptions: The topic editor stores reply parent ids in `discord_messages.reference_id`; the new public tool output should call this `reply_to_message_id` to match the brief and existing topic-editor context output.
`scope` defaults to `window`, preserving the existing source-window-only search behavior unless the agent explicitly asks for `archive`.

`has=['file']` means any non-empty attachment; `image`, `video`, and `audio` are derived from attachment content type or filename extension; `embed` means non-empty embeds; `link` means URL-like text in `content`.
If a dedicated mentions column is absent, `mentions_author_id` can be implemented by matching Discord mention tokens in message content for window scope and archive scope.
Archive range clamping means the effective interval should not exceed 30 days; malformed time syntax is still an error.
The existing `_tool_result_content(...)` JSON truncation is acceptable as the standard ~2KB cap pattern, but helper-level `content_preview` trimming should reduce how often it truncates blindly.
Tests should be concentrated in `tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py` as requested; broader storage fake tests are useful only if they stay cheap and reliable.
